name: WILSON DISEASE
definition: HEPATOLENTICULAR DEGENERATION autosomal recessive disorder with increased intestinal resorption of copper → excessive excessive copper deposition in brain

demographics.Location: commonly in lenticular nucleus (= lenslike configuration of putamen + globus

distribution: commonly in lenticular nucleus (= lenslike configuration of putamen + globus
discriminating_features: areas of CT hypodensities + T2 prolongation
normal hepatic attenuation (fatty infiltration + copper deposition cancel each other out)
normal T1 relaxation time (in spite of paramagnetic effects of copper)

CNS: areas of CT hypodensities + T2 prolongation
Liver: normal hepatic attenuation (fatty infiltration + copper deposition cancel each other out)
normal T1 relaxation time (in spite of paramagnetic effects of copper)
Skeletal manifestations (in ⅔): premature osteoarthritis (narrowing of joint space + osteophyte formation)
premature osteoarthrosis of spine, prominent Schmorl nodes, wedging of vertebrae,
generalized deossification may produce pathologic fractures

clinical.Rx: life-long pharmacologic therapy with chelation agents (penicillamine / trientine / zinc);
